# Clerkk: High Level Design

# Authentication & Onboarding Architecture:

Clerkk uses an invest-time-then-gate onboarding strategy to maximize conversion while maintaining security. Users begin by entering their financial profile (income, expenses, debt, location) through a multi-step form, with all data stored locally on the device. No server communication occurs during this phase, eliminating security risks associated with anonymous user data. Upon form completion, users encounter a sign-up gate requiring authentication via social login (Google, Apple). Once authenticated, the locally-stored profile data is transmitted to the server in a single batch request, where it undergoes tax calculation processing using Canadian tax brackets and returns personalized financial insights (surplus, RRSP/TFSA optimization, health score). This approach leverages sunk cost psychology—users who invest 3 minutes completing the form are 60-80% more likely to sign up to see their results—while avoiding the architectural complexity and security concerns of anonymous UUID-based profiles. All subsequent interactions require JWT-based authentication, ensuring financial data is only stored server-side for committed, identifiable users.

# User Profile Data Model:

Clerkk's data model is designed around a core customer insight: high-earning Canadians ($100k-$250k) want financial clarity without the friction of daily transaction tracking. We collect only what's necessary to answer the customer's fundamental question: "What can I do with my money this month?" The profile consists of three pillars that directly map to customer decision-making:

• **Income**: gross annual salary and monthly net pay, because customers think in terms of what actually hits their account
• **Expenses**: fixed costs (rent, utilities, groceries, miscellaneous) that remain stable until life changes, plus optional variable expense logging only when customers exceed their budget, respecting their time by avoiding micro-management
• **Debt**: credit cards, mortgages, and other obligations that constrain available surplus

We also collect location (province/territory) to ensure tax calculations reflect the customer's actual CRA bracket. This minimal structure enables server-side computation of monthly surplus, RRSP/TFSA optimization, and financial health scoring delivering actionable guidance in under 3 minutes of setup. By rejecting the industry norm of exhaustive transaction tracking, we eliminate the primary reason customers abandon financial apps: cognitive overhead that exceeds perceived value.
